book: UML Diagramming A Case Study Approach
author: Suriya Sundaramoorthy
requirement_raw:
The ‘Textile Management System’ case study is designed to monitor all the processes in a tex-
tile manufacturing industry. There are various processes such as order receiving, purchase of raw
materials, classification of raw materials, production of goods and selling the products. The main
aim of this system is to ensure the reliability of data and to ease the process of manufacturing and
transportation of garments and raw materials. This system ensures that the hardships prevailing
in the manual systems are overridden by reducing the errors in data handling. The major func-
tionalities are customer registration, sampling, stock checking, purchasing and processing of raw
materials, production of garments, product checking and clearance, shipment, billing and report
generation (Figures 16.1–16.8).
requirement_paraphrase:
The Textile Management System is developed to monitor and manage all processes in a textile manufacturing industry. The system covers order receiving, purchasing of raw materials, classification and processing of raw materials, production of garments, and selling of finished products.

The objective of the system is to ensure reliable data handling and simplify the manufacturing and transportation processes of garments and raw materials. It reduces the difficulties present in manual systems by minimizing errors in data management.

Functionalities

Customer registration

Receiving garment orders

Sampling

Stock checking

Purchasing raw materials

Processing raw materials

Production of garments

Product checking and clearance

Shipment

Billing

Report generation
